items.title: Lifting and preserving state: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Duplicating shared state in both children is safe if callbacks try to keep it synchronized.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use one common owner for genuinely shared state and pass controlled values rather than synchronizing duplicate copies.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Use one common owner for genuinely shared state and pass controlled values rather than synchronizing duplicate copies.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.
